consistency.level_placement: Consistent (Standard starting feature level for Channel Divinity, though technically granted by the Oath subclass, the core feature is listed here).
consistency.mechanical_description: Accurate representation of the core Channel Divinity mechanic: uses per rest (short/long rest recharge), choice of options, and spell save DC linkage.

quality.completeness: High. Captures the essential rules text for using Channel Divinity.
quality.clarity: High. The description is clear regarding usage limits and recharge.

mechanics.scaling_interaction: Directly references 'paladin spell save DC' for relevant effects.
mechanics.prerequisite: Implicitly requires choosing an Oath (as noted by 'option provided by your oath').
mechanics.notes: This entry represents the general class feature that allows the use of Oath-specific abilities. It is the framework, not the specific options.

potential_issues: The 'level' being listed as 1 might be slightly misleading if this data model assumes features are listed at the level they are gained. Channel Divinity is usually gained via the Oath subclass feature at level 3, even though the class feature itself exists conceptually earlier. However, in many digital reference formats, the core mechanic might be listed at level 1 if it is the base capability upon which subclasses build.

llm_audit.archivist_notes: The data is mechanically sound and accurately reflects the core rules text for the Paladin's Channel Divinity resource. Consistency is high across metadata fields. Minor ambiguity exists regarding the 'level: 1' placement relative to when the feature is typically unlocked (Level 3 Oath feature), but this is likely an artifact of the source data structure rather than an error in the text itself.
